Metal: Mn (II)
Fe (II)
Co (II)
Zn (II)
Ni (II)
Cu (II)
Notes
[*] Estimated from the Irving-Williams order (Mn < Fe < Co < Ni < Cu > Zn); Fe(II)-GSH = 5.1 [1] is the nearest measured value; no primary aqueous measurement of Co(II)-GSH log K was located.

[†] Cu(II) values; Cu(II) can oxidise free thiols in vivo. The physiologically relevant cytosolic species is Cu(I): Cu(I)-GSH log K ~12-16; Cu(I)-Cys log K ~14-17 (Corazza A et al. 1996 Eur J Biochem 236:697-702).
Fe(II)-citrate [9] and Fe(II)-ATP [10] are less well-characterised than the corresponding Fe(III) complexes. Values are approximate and taken from speciation analysis of the cytoplasmic iron pool.

GSSG data for Mn / Fe / Co are not tabulated because no reliable primary measurements were located; those metals bind GSSG only weakly (no free thiol) and the data are absent from standard compilations.
References
[1] Hider RC & Kong XL (2011) Glutathione: a key component of the cytoplasmic labile iron pool. BioMetals 24:1179-1187
[2] Clough et al. (2025) bioRxiv
[3] Foster AW et al. (2022) Metallomics 14
[4] Lacasse MJ & Zamble DB (2023) Direct Detection of the Labile Nickel Pool in Escherichia coli: New Perspectives on Labile Metal Pools. JACS
[5] Martell AE & Smith RM (1974) Critical Stability Constants Vol 1: Amino Acids. Plenum Press New York
[6] Williams DR (1970) Stability constants and thermodynamic parameters for Mn(II) Fe(II) Co(II) Ni(II) Cu(II) Zn(II) with histidine and tryptophan. J Chem Soc A 1550-1555

[7] Wyrzykowski D & Chmurzyński L (2010) Thermodynamics of citrate complexation with Mn2+ Co2+ Ni2+ and Zn2+ ions. J Therm Anal Calorim 102:61-64. Values cited therein for log K(M2+ + HCit3- -> [MHCit]-) at 298 K

[8] Perrin DD & Sharma VS (1966) The stability constants of metal-adenosine triphosphate complexes. Biochim Biophys Acta 127:35-41. Confirmed by Sigel H (1987) Isomeric equilibria in complexes of ATP with divalent metal ions. Eur J Biochem 165:65-72

[9] Approximate log K1 for Fe(II) + Cit3- -> [FeCit]-; Fe(III)-citrate log K >> 11. Fe(II) value inferred from Pham AN & Waite TD (2008) Oxygenation of Fe(II) in presence of citrate. J Phys Chem A 112:643-651

[10] Approximate log K1 for Fe(II)-ATP; Fe(II) not covered in Sigel 1987. Value estimated from cytoplasmic iron speciation analysis in Hider RC & Kong XL (2013) Iron speciation in the cytosol: an overview. Dalton Trans 42:3220-3229
[11] Fuhr BJ & Rabenstein DL (1973) NMR studies of the solution chemistry of metal complexes: binding of Cd Zn Pb and Hg by glutathione. J Am Chem Soc 95:6944-6948
[12] Krezel A et al. (2011) Zn(II) complexes of glutathione disulfide: structural basis of elevated stabilities. Inorg Chem 50:72-85

[13] Estimated from potentiometric and NMR data on Cu(II)-GSSG complexes; Postal M et al. (1985) The binding of copper(II) and zinc(II) to oxidized glutathione. Inorg Chim Acta 108:L19-L21; Ruzza P et al. (2004) Biopolymers 75:154-162
